title: GCP Security Tasks — Infrastructure Changes
context: These tasks must be run on GCP infrastructure (gcloud CLI, Cloud Console). They complement the code changes already applied locally for SEC-002/003/004/008/012/013. Deploy the code first, then apply these tasks in phase order.

tasks.title: Create dedicated service accounts + remove roles/editor
commands: gcloud iam service-accounts create rdf-api-sa --display-name='RDF API Cloud Run'
gcloud iam service-accounts create rdf-batch-sa --display-name='RDF Batch VM'
gcloud run services update rdf-api --region europe-central2 --service-account=rdf-api-sa@rdf-api-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
# --- Remove overly broad role (only after verifying services work!) ---
verification: gcloud run services describe rdf-api --region europe-central2 --format='value(spec.template.spec.serviceAccountName)'
tasks.note: Also resolves SEC-011 (overly broad storage-full scope).

tasks.title: Tighten CORS to actual frontend domains
commands: gcloud run services update rdf-api --region europe-central2 --set-env-vars 'CORS_ORIGINS=["https://joy-forge-express.lovable.app","https://aaed77c9-b864-4814-8747-ad97402d1f70.lovableproject.com"]'

tasks.title: Lock down GCS buckets — prevent public access
Require SSL on Cloud SQL connections
tasks.note: Cloud Run (Auth Proxy) is unaffected. If batch VM still uses public IP, add sslmode=verify-full to DATABASE_URL.

tasks.title: Install Cloud SQL Auth Proxy on batch VM + remove public IP
tasks.depends_on: GCP-SEC-001 (batch VM SA needs cloudsql.client role)
commands: gcloud compute ssh rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a -- 'sudo curl -o /usr/local/bin/cloud-sql-proxy https://storage.googleapis.com/cloud-sql-connectors/cloud-sql-proxy/v2.14.3/cloud-sql-proxy.linux.amd64 && sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/cloud-sql-proxy'
# Install the systemd service (already in deploy/cloud-sql-proxy.service)
gcloud compute ssh rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a -- 'sudo cp /tmp/cloud-sql-proxy.service /etc/systemd/system/ && sudo systemctl daemon-reload && sudo systemctl enable --now cloud-sql-proxy'
# Update .env: change DATABASE_URL to use localhost
gcloud compute ssh rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a -- 'sudo systemctl restart krs-scanner rdf-worker'
# After verifying proxy works, remove public IP
verification: gcloud compute ssh rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a -- 'sudo systemctl status cloud-sql-proxy'
gcloud compute ssh rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a -- 'sudo systemctl status krs-scanner'

tasks.title: Switch batch VM to IAP tunneling + remove external IP
commands: # Cloud NAT for outbound internet (workers need ms.gov.pl)
gcloud compute routers nats create rdf-nat --router=rdf-router --region=europe-central2 --nat-all-subnet-ip-ranges --auto-allocate-nat-external-ips
gcloud compute instances delete-access-config rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a --access-config-name='External NAT'
tasks.note: After this, always SSH via: gcloud compute ssh rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a --tunnel-through-iap

tasks.title: Deploy secrets wrapper to batch VM + remove secrets from .env
commands: # Deploy updated systemd services + wrapper script
gcloud compute scp deploy/krs-scanner.service deploy/rdf-worker.service deploy/metadata-backfill.service rdf-batch-vm:/tmp/ --zone=europe-central2-a

gcloud compute ssh rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a -- 'sudo cp /tmp/krs-scanner.service /tmp/rdf-worker.service /tmp/metadata-backfill.service /etc/systemd/system/ && sudo systemctl daemon-reload'
# Remove secrets from .env (keep only non-sensitive vars)
gcloud compute ssh rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a -- 'sudo systemctl restart krs-scanner rdf-worker'
verification: gcloud compute ssh rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a -- 'sudo systemctl status krs-scanner'

tasks.title: Enable Cloud SQL automated backups
tasks.note: Replaces the stale DuckDB cron (already removed from deploy/rdf-backup.cron in code).

tasks.title: Enable Cloud Audit Logs for data access
verification: gcloud projects get-iam-policy rdf-api-project --format=json | grep -A5 auditConfigs
tasks.note: Start with DATA_WRITE only to minimize Cloud Logging costs. Add DATA_READ later if needed.

execution_order.description: Deploy the codebase with SEC-002/003/004/008/012/013 fixes.

execution_order.phase: Phase 1 — IAM + network hardening (independent, any order)
execution_order.description: Dedicated SAs, tighten CORS, lock GCS, require SSL. All independent.
Cloud SQL proxy on VM, IAP tunneling, secrets from Secret Manager. Requires Phase 1 SAs.
Automated backups, audit logs. Lower priority.
